name: Sensors & Physical Measurement
description: Sub-sector template for sensor and physical measurement patents (CPC G01D*, G01K*, G01R19*) covering temperature sensors, voltage/current measurement, and transducer signal conditioning.
contextDescription: Sensor and measurement patents cover on-chip and system-level monitoring of physical quantities. Key areas include temperature sensors (on-die thermal management), voltage/current monitors (power management), and sensor signal conditioning (IoT, automotive). On-chip sensors are in every modern processor for thermal throttling and power management. High-value targets include all processor/SoC vendors (Intel, AMD, Apple, Qualcomm), power management IC vendors (TI, Analog Devices, ON Semi), automotive sensor vendors (NXP, Infineon, Bosch), and IoT chipset vendors.
scoringGuidance: On-die temperature sensors are in every modern processor — absolutely ubiquitous.
Voltage/current monitoring patents target power management in all electronic systems.
Sensor interface circuits (AFE) are the bridge between physical world and digital — critical for IoT.
High-accuracy measurement patents target industrial and automotive applications.
Low-power sensor circuits target wearable and always-on IoT devices.

questions.question: What physical quantity does this patent measure or sense? Score by market prevalence: 1-3 for specialized (magnetic, pressure), 4-6 for common (light, humidity), 7-10 for ubiquitous (temperature, voltage/current — in every chip).
questions.reasoningPrompt: Identify the measurement type and how many electronic systems require it.

questions.question: Is this sensor/monitor designed for on-chip integration (on-die temperature, voltage droop detector)? On-chip sensors read on all SoC vendors vs external sensors that target fewer vendors.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Assess whether this is integrated on-chip or a standalone measurement device.

questions.question: Does this patent improve measurement accuracy, resolution, or calibration? Higher accuracy enables premium applications (medical, industrial, automotive).
questions.reasoningPrompt: Identify accuracy or resolution improvements and their application impact.

questions.question: Is this patent relevant to automotive sensing or IoT applications? These are high-growth markets with strong demand for sensor IP.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Identify automotive or IoT applications and growth potential.
